title: Presentation-Logic Split for Server Pages

Presentation-Logic Split for Server Pages

The data structure and layout definitions of all server pages can be defined and stored separately.

Separating the presentation (web layout) from the logic (component structure) provides a great deal of flexibility because the choice of who maintains the layout, and where and how it is maintained, is up to you.

The component data structure is defined and maintained in the Define Structure worksheet of the Component Editor. The layout is defined in the Design Layout worksheet.

Development objects in the component structure must be bound to XHTML elements in the layout. Uniface enables you to copy objects in the structure and paste them as XHTML elements in the layout, with the binding information automatically provided. The XHTML code produced depends on whether you are defining a static or a dynamic server page, primarily because the binding mechanism is different. For more information, see Binding Web Layout to Component Structure.

Web Layouts

Web layouts can be stored in the Repository, or as .hts files. If they are stored as files, multiple files can be used to create a single web page at runtime, for example a header, footer, and body layout. When stored in the Repository, it is only possible to have one web layout per server page.

Web layouts can be maintained using the Uniface IDE or an external XHTML-compliant editor.

This means that the web layout can be put in the hands of Web designers instead of being integrated in the Uniface definitions and editable only by Uniface developers.

Related Links
  • Component Data Structure

    Every component has a data structure that defines its component entities and fields, and the path required to access the data. The data structure affects the validation rules for data I/O, such as enforcing referential integrity constraints.

  • Binding Web Layout to Component Structure

    Binding refers to the way in which layout elements are connected to data structures and triggers in the component structure. It ensures that data is displayed by the appropriate HTML elements in the web page, and that the correct trigger is executed when data in a field is changed.
